2604.19584.title: A Bolu: A Structured Dataset for the Computational Analysis of Sardinian Improvisational Poetry
2604.19584.paper_summary: The growing interest of Natural Language Processing (NLP) in minority languages has not yet bridged the gap in the preservation of oral linguistic heritage. In particular, extemporaneous poetry - a performative genre based on real-time improvisation, metrical-rhetorical competence - remains a largely unexplored area of computational linguistics. This methodological gap necessitates the creation of specific resources to document and analyse the structures of improvised poetry. This is the context in which A Bolu was created, the first structured corpus of extemporaneous poetry dedicated to cantada logudorese, a variant of the Sardinian language. The dataset comprises 2,835 stanzas for a total of 141,321 tokens. The study presents the architecture of the corpus and applies a multidimensional analysis combining descriptive statistical indices and computational linguistics techniques to map the characteristics of the poetic text. The results indicate that the production of Sardinian extemporaneous poets is characterised by recurring patterns that support Parry and Lord's theory of formulaicity. This evidence not only provides a new key to understanding oral creativity, but also offers a significant contribution to the development of NLP tools that are more inclusive and sensitive to the specificities of less widely spoken languages.
2604.19584.paper_summary_zh: 自然語言處理（NLP）對於少數語言的日益關注尚未填補口頭語言遺產保存的鴻溝。尤其是即興詩——一種基於即時即興、韻律修辭能力的表演性類型——仍然是計算語言學中一個大多數未被探索的領域。這一方法論的空白需要創建特定資源來記錄和分析即興詩的結構。這就是《A Bolu》創建的背景，這是第一個專門針對坎塔達·洛古多雷塞語的即興詩結構語料庫，該語言是撒丁語的一個變體。該數據集包含2,835首詩節，共計141,321個語素。該研究展示了語料庫的架構，並應用多維分析，結合描述性統計指標和計算語言學技術，以描繪詩歌文本的特徵。結果表明，撒丁島即興詩人的創作特徵是由重複模式構成，這支持了Parry和Lord的公式性理論。這一證據不僅為理解口頭創造力提供了一個新的關鍵，還對開發更具包容性和敏感性、能夠適應使用較少語言的NLP工具作出了重要貢獻。
2604.19584.author: Silvio Calderaro et.al.
